member.name: T:TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitutions.AssemblyDocumentation
member.summary: Assembly-level documentation for the TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitution library.
member.remarks:
This assembly implements helpers to determine whether a runtime type can be used in place of another type according to the Liskov/Wing Substitution Principle.
IMPORTANT: Throughout this documentation the term "subtype" (and related API names such as "IsVariantOf", "IsSubtypeOf", "IsInstanceOf") is used in the stronger, behavioral sense defined by Liskov and Wing — i.e. a type S is a subtype of type T only if S can be used wherever T is expected without altering desirable program properties. This is NOT merely syntactic (name-based) or structural subtyping (shape/members) nor simply assignability by the CLR; the checks performed by this library take into account generic variance, generic parameter constraints and behavioral substitutability.

member.name: T:TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitutions.TypeExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods to determine whether a type can be used in place of another according to the Liskov/Wing Substitution Principle.
member.remarks: See assembly-level documentation for the clarification of "subtype" semantics used by these APIs.

member.name: M:TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitutions.TypeExtensions.IsVariantOf(System.Type,System.Type)
member.summary: Determines whether the source type can be considered a Liskov/Wing-style subtype of the target type.
member.param: Target type to check substitutability against.
member.returns: True if the source type is considered a behavioral subtype of the target type; otherwise false.

member.name: M:TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitutions.TypeExtensions.IsVariantOf(System.Type,System.Type,System.Type@)
member.summary: Determines whether the source type can be considered a Liskov/Wing-style subtype of the target type, and returns the runtime type that satisfies the substitutability check when available.
member.param: Target type to check substitutability against.
When this method returns, contains the runtime type that can be used to satisfy expected type, or null if not applicable.
member.returns: True if the source type is considered a behavioral subtype of the target type; otherwise false.

member.name: T:TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitutions.ConversionExtensions
member.summary: Instance conversion helpers built on top of the subtype/variance checks.
member.remarks: These helpers attempt to convert instances to the runtime substitution type discovered by the algorithm. They respect the Liskov/Wing interpretation of subtype used by the library.

member.name: M:TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitutions.ConversionExtensions.ConvertAs``1(System.Object,System.Type)
member.summary: Attempts to convert the instance to an object that satisfies according to the library's substitution rules.
member.param: Target type to convert to (may be a generic definition).
member.returns: A converted instance if conversion is possible; otherwise null.

member.name: T:TypeLogic.LiskovWingSubstitutions.ConversionInfo
member.summary: Internal type describing a discovered conversion and a lazily-built converter delegate.
member.remarks: Used internally by caching and conversion helpers. The documented "runtime type" stored here is the closed generic or constructed runtime type used for substitutability checks.
